questions.q: In which century did Madhava of Sangamagrama live and work?
questions.explanation: Madhava lived roughly from 1340 to 1425 CE, placing him in the 14th and early 15th centuries.

questions.q: Madhava is regarded as the founder of which influential school of mathematics and astronomy?
questions.explanation: Madhava founded the Kerala School of astronomy and mathematics, which flourished for centuries after him.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state did Madhava and his school flourish?
questions.explanation: Madhava and the Kerala School were based in the region of present-day Kerala in southern India.

questions.q: Madhava is most famous for discovering infinite series for which mathematical constant?
questions.explanation: Madhava found infinite series expansions that allow pi to be computed to high accuracy.

questions.q: Madhava's discoveries are widely seen as anticipating which branch of mathematics, developed in Europe centuries later?
questions.explanation: His work on infinite series and power series anticipated key ideas of calculus by roughly 250 years.

questions.q: Besides pi, Madhava developed infinite series for which kind of functions?
questions.explanation: Madhava derived power-series expansions for trigonometric functions such as sine and cosine.

questions.q: The place name 'Sangamagrama' associated with Madhava refers to what?
options: His town or place of origin
questions.explanation: Sangamagrama denotes the town Madhava was from, generally identified with a place near present-day Kerala.

questions.q: Roughly how many years before European calculus pioneers did Madhava's series work appear?
questions.explanation: Madhava's series predate the calculus of Newton and Leibniz by about two and a half centuries.

questions.q: Madhava's main field of study combined mathematics with which other discipline?
questions.explanation: Like the Kerala School broadly, Madhava worked in both mathematics and astronomy.

questions.q: What kind of mathematical expression, summing infinitely many terms, is central to Madhava's pi and sine results?
questions.explanation: Madhava expressed pi and trigonometric functions as infinite series of terms.

questions.q: The series pi/4 = 1 - 1/3 + 1/5 - 1/7 + ... is often named after Madhava and which European mathematician?
questions.explanation: This alternating series is commonly called the Madhava-Leibniz series, as Leibniz rediscovered it in Europe.

questions.q: The power series for sine and cosine that Madhava discovered are today usually attributed in the West to which mathematician?
questions.explanation: These are now known as the Taylor (Maclaurin) series for sine and cosine, which Madhava had already found.

questions.q: Which prominent later astronomer of the Kerala School wrote works that preserved and credited Madhava's results?
questions.explanation: Nilakantha Somayaji, a key Kerala School figure, cited Madhava and recorded many of his discoveries.

questions.q: Madhava's arctangent series can be written as arctan(x) = x - x^3/3 + x^5/5 - ... This is the basis for computing pi by setting x equal to what value?
questions.explanation: Setting x = 1 in the arctangent series gives arctan(1) = pi/4, yielding the Madhava-Leibniz series.

questions.q: A major text that transmitted Kerala School mathematics, including Madhava's series, was the Yuktibhasa, written by whom?
questions.explanation: Jyeshtadeva's Yuktibhasa (c. 1530) systematically explained and proved many results originating with Madhava.

questions.q: Madhava devised correction terms added to the partial sums of his pi series in order to do what?
options: Speed up convergence and improve accuracy
Convert it into a finite sum
questions.explanation: Madhava added end-correction terms to the slowly converging series so fewer terms gave more accurate values of pi.

questions.q: Why are very few works directly written by Madhava himself available to historians today?
options: Most are known through quotations by his successors
They were translated into Latin and lost
He wrote only in a secret code
questions.explanation: Most of Madhava's results survive through citations and elaborations by later Kerala School mathematicians rather than his own surviving texts.

questions.q: Madhava produced a notably accurate value of pi correct to roughly how many decimal places?
questions.explanation: Using his series and corrections, Madhava gave a value of pi accurate to about eleven decimal places.

questions.q: The Kerala School compiled accurate tables of which trigonometric quantities, building on Madhava's series?
questions.explanation: Madhava's series let the Kerala School compute highly accurate sine (and cosine) tables.

questions.q: In which language were Madhava's and the Kerala School's mathematical works primarily composed?
questions.explanation: The works were written in Sanskrit, often verse, with later commentaries such as the Yuktibhasa in Malayalam.

questions.q: Madhava's contributions place him at the start of a lineage of mathematicians spanning which approximate period?
questions.explanation: The Kerala School he founded was active from roughly the 14th through the 16th centuries.

questions.q: Which of these is NOT something Madhava is credited with developing?
options: Power series for sine and cosine
Correction terms to improve series convergence
questions.explanation: The decimal place-value system predates Madhava by many centuries; his fame rests on infinite series, not the numeral system.

questions.q: Madhava's series for arctangent, sine, and cosine are special cases of what modern concept?
questions.explanation: Each of Madhava's expansions is a power series, i.e. a Taylor-type expansion of the respective function.

questions.q: The honorific 'Golavid', sometimes linked to Madhava, indicates expertise in what?
questions.explanation: 'Golavid' means master of the sphere, reflecting Madhava's standing in spherical astronomy.

questions.q: Madhava's town Sangamagrama is most commonly identified with which locality in Kerala?
questions.explanation: Sangamagrama is generally identified with the area of Irinjalakuda in central Kerala.

questions.q: Beyond pure mathematics, Madhava applied his methods to compute accurate positions of which celestial bodies?
questions.explanation: Madhava used his trigonometric series to improve calculations of the positions of the Moon and planets.

questions.q: Which of these results is specifically associated with Madhava's name in the history of analysis?
questions.explanation: The arctangent series is known as the Madhava-Gregory (or Madhava-Gregory-Leibniz) series after its independent rediscoverers.

questions.q: What estimate of Madhava's birth and death years is most commonly cited by historians?
questions.explanation: Madhava is generally dated to about 1340-1425 CE; the other ranges belong to Aryabhata, Bhaskara II, and Brahmagupta.

questions.q: One title attributed to Madhava is a work on the Moon's positions. What general theme do his attributed works share?
questions.explanation: Madhava's attributed works center on computational astronomy and the trigonometric methods underpinning it.

questions.q: Why is Madhava's work historically significant in the global development of mathematics?
options: It demonstrated infinite-series methods centuries before they appeared in Europe
questions.explanation: Madhava's infinite-series techniques foreshadowed core methods of European calculus by roughly two and a half centuries, marking a major early advance in analysis.
